sprint: Magic Card -- Decorative Sky vs. Recall Credential
task: A direct, user-raised security question: 'why are we showing the pattern on first screen, is that not something personal to kid. thats identification.' Confirmed and found far more widespread than the one screen first flagged: a full audit of every _renderConstellation() call site found the real, literal recall pattern (the exact tap-credential recall_magic_card() checks against) was being rendered on four passive, zero-interaction surfaces -- the permanent header badge, Magic Card Home's own top sky, the automatic boot-time Welcome-back gate (the one the user flagged, shown to anyone who opens the app), and the Shared Device picker's tiles. Only the ceremony's one-time genesis reveal and Magic Card Home's explicit View My Card action were ever meant to show the real thing. Fixed with a real data-model separation, not a UI patch: js/magicCard.js gained decorativeSkyFor(card), a small deterministic string-seeded generator reusing the exact existing constellation-placement algorithm to derive a SEPARATE, non-secret constellation from the card's own already-public id -- every ambient surface now renders this instead of the real pattern, while the two legitimate reveal moments are untouched. Also implemented the reveal-gate for View My Card that had been designed but not yet written before the interruption: the Back face (the real pattern) now renders blurred behind a Tap to Reveal overlay on Magic Card Home, with Download Back/Print disabled until revealed, plus a warning note -- the ceremony's own First Claimed Moment stays unchanged, the one legitimate save-it-now moment. Verified via a new 9-assertion Playwright suite reading actual rendered SVG coordinates: all four ambient surfaces now show a genuinely different, but stable and deterministic, set of points than the real credential; the reveal-gate works correctly. Full regression across all three existing Magic Card suites (51 checks) and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged. A separate, adjacent bug was found and disclosed but not fixed in this pass: the human-readable code printed on the card doesn't match the format the recall RPC's typed-code fallback actually expects.
